title: Enactment of moral education: Chinese teachers' struggles and reflexivity in practice
abstract: This study explores the challenges Chinese teachers face in moral education, focusing on how they negotiate institutional constraints while striving to uphold their ethical and pedagogical values. Through ethnographic fieldwork in a Chinese school, the research identifies three modes of teacher reflexivity in moral education: conforming with discomfort, caring as emotional labour, and carving an alternative path. These modes reflect different practice logics, ranging from compliance driven by emotional discomfort to affective investment in student relationships, and strategic reinterpretations of institutional norms. The study underscores the tension between institutional demands and personal values, advocating for a shift in moral education away from managerial discourses and affective discourses towards educational discourses grounded in moral judgements, responsibilities, and actions within interpersonal interactions.
journal: British Journal of Sociology of Education
